name: Produce blog drafts with PostgreSQL and WordPress and log the results

options.systemMessage: You are the coordinator for a newsletters pipeline. Break the request into sub-tasks, delegate each to the matching specialist tool, then merge results into one coherent answer. Never call the same specialist twice for one request.

parameters.description: Delegate research sub-tasks for a draft to the Research specialist workflow.
Delegate draft sub-tasks for a draft to the Draft specialist workflow.
Delegate review sub-tasks for a draft to the Review specialist workflow.

parameters.query:

VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.text: =✅ Produce blog drafts with PostgreSQL and WordPress and log the results — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 2 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Frame the Assignment

### Frame the Assignment

Shapes the item: writes `assignment`, `deadline`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Coordinator Agent

### Coordinator Agent

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Next: Unpack Sections

### OpenAI Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `gpt-4o-mini`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Research Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Draft Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Review Specialist (Sub-workflow)

Specialist sub-workflow exposed as a callable tool — one narrow capability per tool.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `tool`

### Enforce Result Schema

Forces the agent's answer into the JSON schema below — downstream nodes rely on this exact shape.

Feeds **Coordinator Agent** as `outputParser`

### Unpack Sections

Explodes the `output.sections` array into one item per element.

Next: Persist Deliverable to PostgreSQL

### Persist Deliverable to PostgreSQL

Runs a parameterized SQL statement against Postgres — values are bound via `queryReplacement`, not string-interpolated, so it's immune to SQL injection.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#content`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Produce blog drafts with PostgreSQL and WordPress and log the results

**Pattern:** Content Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Produce blog drafts with PostgreSQL and WordPress and log the results. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual newsletters is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Frame the Assignment → Coordinator Agent → Unpack Sections → Persist Deliverable to PostgreSQL → Send Summary to Slack

## Use it for
- Teams that want to content generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating newsletters in content creation
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: OpenAI API key; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The Coordinator Agent never does the specialist work itself — each specialist is delegated to its own sub-workflow via a Workflow Tool, so every specialist stays single-responsibility and testable in isolation instead of one giant prompt trying to do everything. An output parser forces the final answer into JSON so downstream nodes can rely on the shape instead of parsing free text.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a third specialist tool and update the system message to explain when the agent should reach for it.
2. Loosen or tighten the JSON schema on the output parser and see how the agent's final answer shape changes.
3. Replace one placeholder Workflow Tool with a real n8n sub-workflow and measure the round-trip latency.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your newsletters process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
